mission.title: Configure Multi-Tenancy Framework (Three Shades of Isolation)
mission.description: Set up the complete multi-tenancy isolation framework combining OVN-Kubernetes, KubeFlex, K3s, and KubeVirt. Based on the KubeCon Europe 2026 talk 'Three Shades of Isolation: A Multi-Tenancy Fortress' by Braulio Dumba & Paolo Dettori (IBM Research). Provides three isolation layers: control-plane (KubeFlex + K3s), data-plane (KubeVirt), and network (OVN UDN L2/L3).

steps.title: Check existing components
steps.description:
Before installing, check which components are already present on the cluster:
```bash
# Check OVN-Kubernetes
kubectl get pods -A -l app=ovnkube-node --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Check KubeFlex
kubectl get pods -A -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Check KubeVirt
kubectl get pods -A -l app=virt-operator --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l

# Check K3s pods
kubectl get pods -A -l app=k3s --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l
```
> Only install components that are missing (count = 0).

steps.title: Install OVN-Kubernetes with UDN support (Network isolation)
steps.description:
OVN-Kubernetes provides User Defined Networks (UDN) for Layer 2/3 network tenant isolation.

```bash
# Create OVN-Kubernetes namespace
kubectl create namespace ovn-kubernetes

# Deploy OVN-Kubernetes (adjust version as needed)
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml

# Wait for OVN pods to be ready
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app=ovnkube-node -n ovn-kubernetes --timeout=300s
```
> Skip this step if OVN-Kubernetes is already installed.

steps.title: Install KubeFlex (Control-plane isolation)
steps.description:
KubeFlex provides dedicated control planes per tenant.

```bash
helm repo add kubestellar https://kubestellar.github.io/helm
helm repo update
helm install kubeflex kubestellar/kubeflex --namespace kubeflex-system --create-namespace

# Wait for controller
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflex -n kubeflex-system --timeout=120s
```
> Skip if KubeFlex is already installed.

steps.title: Install KubeVirt (Data-plane isolation)
steps.description:
KubeVirt runs VMs as Kubernetes pods so tenants don't share compute nodes.

```bash
# Install KubeVirt operator
export KUBEVIRT_VERSION=$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubevirt-prow/release/kubevirt/kubevirt/stable.txt)
kubectl create -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-operator.yaml
kubectl create -f https://github.com/kubevirt/kubevirt/releases/download/${KUBEVIRT_VERSION}/kubevirt-cr.yaml

# Wait for operator
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pods -l app=virt-operator -n kubevirt --timeout=300s
```
> Skip if KubeVirt is already installed.

steps.title: Create tenant namespaces and UDN networks
steps.description:
Set up the network architecture for the first tenant with dual UDN (L3 primary + L2 secondary):

```bash
# Create tenant namespaces
kubectl create namespace tenant-1-ns1
kubectl create namespace tenant-1-ns2

# Label for tenant association
kubectl label namespace tenant-1-ns1 tenant=tenant-1
kubectl label namespace tenant-1-ns2 tenant=tenant-1

# Create Layer-3 UDN (primary — data-plane traffic)
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8s.ovn.org/v1
kind: UserDefinedNetwork
metadata:
  name: tenant-1-l3-primary
  namespace: tenant-1-ns1
spec:
  topology: Layer3
  layer3:
    role: Primary
    subnets:
      - cidr: 10.100.0.0/16
EOF

# Create Layer-2 UDN (secondary — control-plane traffic)
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8s.ovn.org/v1
kind: ClusterUserDefinedNetwork
metadata:
  name: tenant-1-l2-secondary
spec:
  namespaceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      tenant: tenant-1
  network:
    topology: Layer2
    layer2:
      role: Secondary
      subnets:
        - cidr: 10.200.0.0/16
EOF
```
> This creates the dual-network architecture: L3 for data-plane, L2 for control-plane.

steps.title: Create KubeFlex control plane for the tenant
steps.description:
Provision a dedicated control plane for the tenant:

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: tenancy.kubeflex.io/v1alpha1
kind: ControlPlane
metadata:
  name: tenant-1
spec:
  type: k8s
  backend: shared
EOF

# Wait for control plane to be ready
kubectl wait --for=jsonpath='{.status.phase}'=Ready controlplane/tenant-1 --timeout=120s
```
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: tenancy.kubeflex.io/v1alpha1
kind: ControlPlane
metadata:
  name: tenant-1
spec:
  type: k8s
  backend: shared
EOF

steps.title: Deploy KubeVirt VM in tenant namespace-1
steps.description:
Create a KubeVirt VM in namespace-1 with dual NICs (eth0 → L3 UDN primary, eth1 → L2 UDN secondary):

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubevirt.io/v1
kind: VirtualMachine
metadata:
  name: tenant-1-vm
  namespace: tenant-1-ns1
  labels:
    tenant: tenant-1
spec:
  running: true
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        tenant: tenant-1
        kubevirt.io/vm: tenant-1-vm
      annotations:
        k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: tenant-1-l2-secondary
    spec:
      domain:
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: 2Gi
        devices:
          interfaces:
            - name: default
              masquerade: {}
            - name: l2-secondary
              bridge: {}
          disks:
            - name: containerdisk
              disk:
                bus: virtio
      networks:
        - name: default
          pod: {}
        - name: l2-secondary
          multus:
            networkName: tenant-1-l2-secondary
      volumes:
        - name: containerdisk
          containerDisk:
            image: quay.io/kubevirt/fedora-cloud-container-disk-demo:latest
EOF
```
> The VM gets eth0 on L3 UDN (data-plane) and eth1 on L2 UDN (control-plane).

steps.title: Deploy K3s server pod in tenant namespace-2
steps.description:
Deploy a K3s server pod connected via KubeFlex controller through the default K8s network and L2 UDN secondary:

```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: k3s-server
  namespace: tenant-1-ns2
  labels:
    app: k3s
    tenant: tenant-1
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: k3s
      tenant: tenant-1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: k3s
        tenant: tenant-1
      annotations:
        k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: tenant-1-l2-secondary
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: k3s-server
          image: rancher/k3s:v1.31.0-k3s1
          command: ["k3s", "server", "--disable=traefik"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6443
              name: api
EOF
```
> K3s gets eth0 on default network (managed by KubeFlex) and eth1 on L2 UDN (control-plane traffic).

steps.title: Verify the multi-tenancy stack
steps.description:
Confirm all three isolation layers are operational:

```bash
echo '=== Control-plane isolation (KubeFlex + K3s) ==='
kubectl get controlplanes
kubectl get pods -n tenant-1-ns2 -l app=k3s

echo '=== Data-plane isolation (KubeVirt) ==='
kubectl get vmi -n tenant-1-ns1

echo '=== Network isolation (OVN UDN) ==='
kubectl get userdefinednetworks -A
kubectl get clusteruserdefinednetworks
```

uninstall.title: Remove tenant resources
uninstall.description:
Delete tenant VMs, K3s pods, control planes, and namespaces:
```bash
kubectl delete vm --all -n tenant-1-ns1
kubectl delete deployment k3s-server -n tenant-1-ns2
kubectl delete controlplane tenant-1
kubectl delete namespace tenant-1-ns1 tenant-1-ns2
```

troubleshooting.title: VM not getting L2 secondary network
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClusterUserDefinedNetwork may not have matched the namespace labels.

**Fix:** Verify namespace labels match the selector:
```bash
kubectl get namespace tenant-1-ns1 --show-labels
kubectl describe clusteruserdefinednetwork tenant-1-l2-secondary
```

troubleshooting.title: K3s pod not connecting to KubeFlex control plane
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeFlex controller may not have provisioned the control plane service yet.

**Fix:** Check control plane status and K3s logs:
```bash
kubectl get controlplanes
kubectl logs -l app=k3s -n tenant-1-ns2
```

resolution.summary: A successful deployment shows: KubeFlex control plane in Ready state, KubeVirt VM running in tenant-1-ns1, K3s server pod running in tenant-1-ns2, and UDN networks assigned to both namespaces. All three isolation layers (control-plane, data-plane, network) are operational.

prerequisites.resources: Minimum 8 vCPU, 16GB RAM (hosting cluster with VMs and nested clusters)
prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster (OpenShift, EKS, GKE, or similar) with sufficient resources for running VMs and nested control planes.
